prompt: Name one commonly-praised idea, trend, or practice in modern knowledge work that you think is overrated, and explain why.
response: One commonly-praised idea in modern knowledge work that I think is overrated is **open-plan offices**. While they are often celebrated for fostering collaboration, transparency, and flexibility, in practice, open-plan offices frequently lead to increased distractions, reduced concentration, and lower overall productivity. Many workers report higher stress levels and less privacy, which can hamper deep, focused work that knowledge tasks often require. The trade-off between easier communication and disrupted work time often doesn’t justify the loss in employee satisfaction and effectiveness.
